tagline: Precision oncology targeting tumor-specific epitopes to eliminate on-target/off-tumor toxicity

tagline: AI research lab that saturated ARC-AGI-2 at 97.9% for $11.77/task
founders: AI/ML research team (YC W26; founders in stealth)
what: AI research lab building systems optimized for learning efficiency – AI that designs experiments in data-sparse domains and learns maximally from limited data. First result: SOTA on ARC-AGI-2 at 97.9%, open-sourced.
why: ARC-AGI-2 is the hardest benchmark for general AI intelligence. Near-saturation at extremely low cost suggests a qualitatively different approach to learning. In the same batch as Ndea (Chollet), creating a flywheel around AGI measurement.
signal: YC W26; ARC-AGI-2 SOTA (97.9% at $11.77/task); open-sourced solver; adjacent to Ndea (François Chollet) in same YC batch

tagline: Recovering 30% of stranded GPU power in AI data centers
founders: Tomer Timor (CEO), Edward Kizis (CTO) – founded May 2025, Tel Aviv
what: Platform capturing the 'electrical fingerprint' of AI workloads to give data center operators high-resolution power visibility, predicting millisecond-level fluctuations and adjusting workloads in real time.
why: AI data centers strand up to 30% of contracted electrical capacity due to GPU power surges – a multi-billion dollar inefficiency. As AI infrastructure scales, power optimization becomes critical infrastructure.
signal: Exited stealth March 2026; investors: Glilot Capital, Grove Ventures, Arc VC, Encoded VC; energy + AI infrastructure intersection

tagline: 3D nanomanufacturing at 100nm – building the ASML of next-gen chips
founders: Malcolm Tisdale (CEO, NASA Mars Helicopter engineer, Caltech multi-material manufacturing methods); advisors: Dr. H.-S. Philip Wong (TSMC Chief Scientist), Dr. Julia Greer (Caltech Kavli Nanoscience Institute Director)
what: 3D semiconductor manufacturing platform achieving 100nm resolution across full wafers at up to 90% lower cost than traditional 2D lithography, targeting the next scaling law beyond Moore's Law.
why: 2D lithography is hitting physical limits. 3D chip stacking is the next frontier. NASA-pedigree founder + TSMC Chief Scientist + Caltech Kavli Director on advisory board + NVIDIA letter of intent at YC stage is an extraordinary signal.
signal: YC W26; NVIDIA letter of intent; TSMC Chief Scientist + Caltech Kavli Director advisors; NASA Mars Helicopter engineer founder; Caltech spinout

tagline: AI-native protein characterization platform from Nobel laureate Bertozzi lab
founders: David (CEO, Stanford postdoc, Damon Runyon Cancer Research Fellow, 15yr domain expertise), Andrew (COO, Broad Institute MIT/Harvard + Bertozzi Lab Stanford), Vishnu (CTO, 2x YC founder)
what: AI-native platform automating next-generation protein characterization to radically accelerate drug development, built on science from Carolyn Bertozzi's Nobel Prize-winning lab at Stanford, with 47+ publications.
why: Protein characterization is a multi-step bottleneck in every drug pipeline. The team literally built the science this platform automates – unmatched IP and credibility. Already contracted with multiple top-10 pharma companies at YC stage.
signal: YC W26; Carolyn Bertozzi Nobel laureate lab spinout; multiple top-10 pharma enterprise contracts; Broad Institute + Stanford pedigree; 2x YC co-founder

tagline: Making AlphaFold steerable for pharma drug discovery
founders: Nithin & John (MIT co-founders; met at Biology Olympiad; 4 years MIT roommates; NeurIPS + Nature publications in ML/biology)
what: AI platform making protein structure prediction models like AlphaFold steerable and promptable, enabling pharma to direct molecular design with the same intuitive control as prompting LLMs.
why: AlphaFold solved protein structure but left the design problem unsolved. Making it steerable is the critical next step. Two-person MIT team with NeurIPS + Nature papers at pre-seed is an extremely early, high-quality entry point.
signal: MIT co-founders; NeurIPS + Nature publications; Biology Olympiad background; piloting with early-stage biotechs; extremely early stage

tagline: AI orchestration software for multi-robot military teams
founders: Vanja Videnovic, Matthew Buffa, Michael Irwin (ex-Anduril, DroneShield, Hargrave Technologies; UNSW Defence 10X program)
what: Breaker's platform lets military operators coordinate teams of up to 100 autonomous systems across air, land and sea using voice commands, enabling real-time autonomous orchestration for defense missions.
why: Defense tech is the fastest-growing VC sector, and Breaker holds validation contracts with US SOCOM and DSTA Singapore — rare proof points at this stage. Founders come directly from Anduril, the defining defense tech unicorn.
signal: Bessemer Venture Partners lead; US SOCOM + DSTA Singapore validation contracts

tagline: Agentic AI marketing intelligence platform for mid-market brands
founders: Dutta (CEO, ex-Google DeepMind — led RL & GenAI for Imagen and Gemini), Joe Cheuk (CTO, ex-Staff Engineer at Databricks, Meta, Google Cloud)
what: Pomo continuously monitors competitors, demand signals, creative trends and channel performance to surface actionable priorities, then recommends and automates execution with team-defined guardrails — an AI CMO for mid-market companies.
why: Ex-DeepMind (Imagen/Gemini RL team) × ex-Databricks founding team at only $4.5M seed. Backed by Databricks Ventures plus angels from Google DeepMind and Adobe AI — strategic investors with distribution advantages.
signal: Kindred Ventures lead; Databricks Ventures, SV Angel; ex-DeepMind Imagen/Gemini founders; April 2026 launch

tagline: Foundational generative physics AI model for engineering simulation
founders: Alan Patterson & Darren Garvey (AI industry veterans, Cambridge-founded; backed by Cambridge Innovation Capital)
what: BeyondMath builds generative physics AI models trained on core physical principles, enabling orders-of-magnitude faster engineering simulations for aerospace, automotive, energy and advanced manufacturing — replacing weeks of compute with seconds.
why: Foundation models for physics are a nascent but massive wedge: every manufacturer in the world runs simulations. Cambridge-rooted team backed by CIC and InMotion Ventures (Jaguar Land Rover's VC arm), giving direct access to the automotive market.
signal: Cambridge Innovation Capital lead; InMotion Ventures (JLR); UP.Partners, Insight Partners; Cambridge pedigree

tagline: Real-world human movement data infrastructure for humanoid robots
founders: Anshul (ex-Scale AI data infrastructure) & Lyem Ningthou (built data pipelines for United States Air Force)
what: Asimov collects diverse real-world human movement data from households and businesses to train humanoid robots — delivering thousands of hours per day of varied environmental data that factory-only datasets cannot replicate.
why: The humanoid robotics wave needs training data at massive scale, but no dominant neutral data infrastructure layer exists yet. Asimov is positioned as infrastructure for the whole ecosystem. Ex-Scale AI + US Air Force founder pedigree.
signal: YC W26; ex-Scale AI founder; US Air Force data pipeline experience; ecosystem infrastructure play

tagline: High-DOF robotic hands with co-designed data-collection gloves eliminating the embodiment gap
founders: Quanting (Daniel) Xie (CMU Robotics PhD student, ICRA 2024 paper, ex-Apple manufacturing) & Ryan Xie (MS Robotics U Michigan; ex-Canvas drywall robot startup; founded Ground Robotics at StartX '25)
what: Origami builds high-DOF robot hands with in-joint motors paired with a precisely co-designed data-collection glove, so manipulation data collected by humans translates directly to the deployed robot without embodiment gap. Already selling to Amazon Physical AI Labs.
why: Dexterous manipulation is the hardest unsolved problem in robotics. Hardware-software co-design is a genuine technical moat. CMU Robotics PhD + Michigan MS founding team with Amazon as early customer validates the approach at YC-stage.
signal: YC W26; Amazon Physical AI Labs customer; CMU Robotics PhD founder; ICRA 2024 publication

tagline: Post-silicon nanomaterial chip manufacturing scalable to wafer scale beyond Moore's Law
founders: Seoho Jung (CEO) & team — ETH Zurich and Empa spinout with deep materials science and nanofabrication expertise
what: Chiral develops scalable integration processes for nanomaterials (carbon nanotubes, 2D materials) into next-generation semiconductor devices, making post-silicon chips manufacturable at wafer scale to unlock computing performance beyond the limits of conventional silicon.
why: The semiconductor industry is approaching hard physical limits with silicon — a multi-trillion dollar problem. ETH Zurich + Swiss federal lab Empa spinout with Crane Venture and Quantonation backing signals elite deep-tech credibility and defensible IP.
signal: ETH Zurich + Empa (Swiss federal lab) spinout; Crane Venture Partners lead; Quantonation, HCVC; Innosuisse public funding

what: Foundation builds the Phantom — humanoid robots explicitly designed for defense and unstructured industrial environments, operating without facility modifications. Holds SBIR Phase 3 contracts making it an approved military vendor for the US Army, Navy and Air Force.
tagline: Autonomous humanoid robots purpose-built for defense with US military SBIR approval
founders: Mike LeBlanc (14-year US Marine Corps veteran, multiple combat tours; co-founder with deep defense procurement expertise) & team
why: SBIR Phase 3 status is extraordinarily rare for an early-stage startup — it provides a direct, non-competitive procurement pipeline with the US military. $24M in government research contracts at seed stage is exceptional proof of product-market fit.
signal: SBIR Phase 3 contracts (US Army, Navy & Air Force); $24M in government research contracts; Tribe Capital seed lead

tagline: First-in-class AAV gene therapy reversing the immunological root cause of dry AMD
founders: Ying Kai Chan, PhD (CEO, former CSO at Ally Therapeutics) & Andrew Dick (Co-Founder/CSA, Professor of Ophthalmology, University of Bristol — university spinout)
what: Cirrus develops an AAV gene therapy that restores IRAK-M expression in the eye, targeting the root immunological cause of dry age-related macular degeneration — a disease affecting 200M+ patients globally with no approved disease-modifying treatment.
why: University of Bristol spinout with mechanistically novel, IP-driven approach to the largest unmet need in ophthalmology. Professor Andrew Dick is a world authority in ocular immunology. ClavystBio, the leading specialist ophthalmology VC, led the round.
signal: University of Bristol spinout; ClavystBio lead (top ophthalmology VC); Polaris Partners; Ying Kai Chan PhD CEO

tagline: In vivo partial epigenetic reprogramming gene therapy for Alzheimer's and brain aging
founders: Yuri Deigin (CEO, serial biotech entrepreneur) & João Pedro de Magalhães, PhD (CSO, Professor at University of Birmingham — one of the world's most cited computational aging biologists)
what: YouthBio's lead program YB002 uses transient Yamanaka factor expression (OSKM) to reverse epigenetic aging in the brain, targeting Alzheimer's disease. Received FDA productive INTERACT meeting outcome, paving an IND pathway for the first human partial reprogramming trial in neurodegeneration.
why: João Pedro de Magalhães is among the most cited aging researchers globally; the FDA's constructive INTERACT meeting is exceptional validation for a seed company. Epigenetic reprogramming is the frontier of longevity medicine — first-mover advantage in a field that could define the next decade of biotech.
signal: FDA INTERACT meeting (supportive IND pathway); João Pedro de Magalhães PhD (top global aging researcher) as CSO; Goldcrest Capital backing

tagline: Physics-informed AI platform rationally designing molecular glues for undruggable inflammatory targets
founders: Andrew Potterton & Chris Tame (CEO) — UK drug discovery specialists; advisory board includes Ian Taylor (ex-President R&D, Arvinas)
what: Ternary combines machine learning, physics-based molecular modelling and rapid lab validation to design molecular glues — small molecules that force proximity between two proteins to drive targeted degradation, focusing on inflammatory and neuroinflammatory diseases.
why: Molecular glues address ~80% of the proteome considered undruggable by conventional methods and are one of the hottest modalities in drug discovery. Founded in November 2024 — this is maximum early-stage access for a compelling platform with strong European VC and government backing.
signal: daphni lead (top European biotech VC); UK Innovation & Science Seed Fund; i&i Biotech Fund; Ian Taylor (ex-Arvinas President R&D) on board

tagline: 3D-bioprinted vascularized human tissue implants for wound repair and organ replacement
founders: Alexander Thomas, Tobias Lam & Lutz Kloke — tissue engineering and biofabrication specialists; backed by Germany's Federal Agency for Breakthrough Innovation (SPRIND)
what: Cellbricks engineers vascularized human tissue therapeutics using a proprietary biofabrication platform, with a lead adipose tissue implant program for severe wound and soft tissue defect repair. Long-term roadmap: 3D-bioprinted viable human organs.
why: SPRIND (Germany's DARPA equivalent for breakthrough science) participation alongside Silicon Roundabout Ventures is a rare combination of scientific credibility and commercial backing. Platform approach enables a step-function from implants today to organ replacement tomorrow.
signal: SPRIND (Germany's Federal Breakthrough Innovation Agency) grant participation; Silicon Roundabout Ventures lead; ACT Venture Partners

tagline: Agentic AI platform for enterprise deployment
founders: Neil Lawrence (DeepMind Professor of ML, University of Cambridge; ex-Director of ML at Amazon), Eno Thereska (ex-Distinguished Engineer at Alcion, AWS, Confluent)
what: Building an agentic AI platform targeting the 74% of businesses planning agentic AI deployments, focused on making enterprise AI agents safer and more reliable.
why: Rare combo of a world-class ML academic (DeepMind Professor, high-citation researcher) with deep distributed-systems industry leadership. Lawrence is a widely respected founding figure in European AI.
signal: DeepMind Professor at Cambridge + ex-AWS/Confluent Distinguished Engineer; active European AI capital flowing into agentic deployment

tagline: Developer platform for the AI coding-agent era
founders: Thomas Dohmke (former CEO of GitHub, oversaw rise of GitHub Copilot; ex-Microsoft)
what: Open-source developer tooling (first product Checkpoints) that captures the reasoning and context behind AI-generated code so teams can manage fleets of AI coding agents.
why: Dohmke is the most credentialed dev-tools operator alive right now, with a timely thesis: the volume of agent-generated code has outgrown human-first tooling. Largest-ever dev tools seed round.
signal: Ex-GitHub CEO, led by Felicis, M12 (Microsoft), Madrona, Basis Set at $300M valuation

tagline: Industrial 'nervous system' AI for heavy industry
founders: Founding team with deep physical AI + industrial automation backgrounds
what: AI-native observability/control layer for industrial facilities — ingesting sensor and process data to coordinate operations in real time.
why: Small, truly early stage (pre-Series A) with a defensible physical-AI wedge. Industrial AI is one of the last greenfield applications of foundation models.
signal: Funded seed round, TechCrunch coverage in Jan 2026 as early pick in industrial-AI thesis

tagline: Faster, cheaper CI/CD runners for AI-native dev teams
founders: Technical founding team building infra for modern software delivery
what: Drop-in GitHub Actions runner replacement that accelerates CI workloads, aimed at teams increasingly shipping AI-assisted code at higher volume.
why: Series A closed in 14 days, 4 months after a $3.5M seed — rare velocity signal. Total raised well under $15M, still firmly early stage. GV double-down is meaningful conviction.
signal: Google Ventures led Series A just 4 months post-seed

tagline: 'Claude Code for Science' — agentic research automation
founders: Ishaan Gangwani and Aayam Bansal (technical founders, raised $1.4M pre-seed before YC)
what: AI agents that autonomously design, run, and iterate on scientific experiments — extending the Claude Code paradigm from software engineering to wet-lab and computational science.
why: Pre-YC traction ($1.4M before batch started) signals investor conviction. Sits at the intersection of the two biggest themes: agents + science. Extremely accessible stage.
signal: Y Combinator W26 + $1.4M pre-seed before batch
source: American Bazaar / YC W26 Demo Day coverage

tagline: Wearable hardware AI that records & summarizes real-world conversations
founders: Akshay Narisetti (CEO, SF-based hardware/AI founder, co-founder since 2024)
what: Dedicated device plus AI stack that captures, transcribes and summarizes in-person conversations — moving conversational AI from screens to ambient hardware.
why: YC W26 — the strongest batch in YC history (35% in top 20% of all YC companies ever). Hardware AI is only ~14% of the batch, making this a scarce physical-product bet.
signal: Y Combinator W26, highlighted as one of 16 most-interesting in batch

tagline: Agentic drug discovery company simulating human biology
founders: David van Dijk (Yale Associate Professor, 11,000+ citations, Cell/Nature/ICML/NeurIPS; turned down Google to start this), Ivan Vrkic (co-built core tech at Yale, previously ran LHC control software at CERN + led foundation-model training at a biotech)
what: Foundation models (Cell2Sentence, co-developed with Google DeepMind) plus AI agents that autonomously run the full drug-discovery pipeline for pharma partners.
why: Textbook exceptional-founder set-up: top-10% computational-biology professor + CERN/foundation-model engineer, both Yale-anchored, with academic IP already published at ICML and commercial MOU with Senhwa Biosciences.
signal: YC W26 + Yale Professor with 11,000+ citations + signed Top-10 pharma MOU

tagline: AI-enabled enzyme and targeted drug design platform
founders: Weston Kightlinger, PhD (CEO, co-founder & ex-CTO of SwiftScale Biologics, acquired by Resilience), Mike Jewett (Stanford Professor of Bioengineering, academic co-founder), Grant Landwehr PhD (ex-Stanford), Francesca Li PhD (ex-Caltech)
what: ML-accelerated cell-free biology platform engineering enzymes and conjugates for ADCs, in vivo CAR-T, radiotherapeutics, and targeted nucleic acid delivery.
why: Rare stack: Stanford professor + Caltech/Stanford PhDs + a repeat biotech founder whose last company was acquired. Strong IP moat around cell-free enzyme engineering.
signal: Stanford faculty co-founder + prior biotech exit (SwiftScale → Resilience); oversubscribed seed

tagline: SureTAC targeted membrane protein degradation for oncology & autoimmunity
founders: Prof. Madelon Maurice (UMC Utrecht, Oncode Institute investigator; SureTAC invention), Neill Moray Mackenzie (founder/MD, serial biotech exec, ex-Wellcome plc)
what: Bispecific antibodies that pair an optimal E3 ligase with a disease-causing target protein to ubiquitinate and lysosomally degrade it — a new modality targeting 'undruggable' membrane proteins.
why: Oncode Institute + Kurma Partners/Argobio spinout — Dutch protein-degradation IP is an area with multiple biotech billion-dollar outcomes. Professor-founder with deep academic lineage.
signal: Oncode Institute spinout + Kurma/Biovance Capital co-lead

tagline: Autologous neuron replacement for Parkinson's disease
founders: Ole Isacson MD PhD (Founding Director, Neuroregeneration Research Institute, McLean Hospital/Harvard Medical School; 30+ years of Parkinson's research), Nikola Kojic MD PhD; Ron Cohen MD (CEO, founder of public biotech Acorda Therapeutics)
what: Autologous stem-cell-derived neuron replacement therapy for Parkinson's and other neurodegenerative disorders, spun out of Harvard/Mass General Brigham research.
why: Founding scientist is a Harvard Medical School/McLean institute director; CEO is a commercially proven CNS biotech builder (Acorda). Total raised ~$42M keeps it firmly early-stage.
signal: Harvard Medical School / McLean Hospital spinout + Acorda founder as CEO

tagline: CAR-T cell therapy for glioblastoma brain tumors
founders: Nick Leschly (founder/CEO, former CEO of bluebird bio — took it public, multiple approved gene therapies); tech licensed from Massachusetts General Hospital
what: Commercializing a next-generation CAR-T platform developed at MGH aimed at glioblastoma — historically one of the hardest tumors to treat.
why: Nick Leschly is a proven biotech CEO with approved therapies under his belt; pairing him with MGH IP on a high-unmet-need indication is an unusually pedigreed seed.
signal: Ex-bluebird bio CEO + MGH IP + ARCH Venture lead

tagline: Small-molecule proteasome activators for neurodegeneration
founders: Dr. Diogo Feleciano, Prof. Dr. Darci Trader (academic co-founder); launched out of Apollo Health Ventures' company creation program
what: DGRADX platform designs small molecules that directly activate 20S proteasomes, restoring cellular clearance of disease-causing proteins — initial focus Parkinson's and Alzheimer's.
why: Apollo/Novo Holdings-backed creation with a professor co-founder and a genuinely differentiated modality (proteasome activators, not inhibitors). Small seed keeps entry price attractive.
signal: Apollo Health Ventures + Novo Holdings co-creation + professor co-founder

tagline: Vertical GaN transistors for AI chip power delivery
founders: Dr. Josh Perozek (MIT PhD), Cynthia Liao, and Prof. Tomás Palacios (MIT EECS, MTL Director)
what: MIT spinout building vertical gallium nitride transistors that push energy conversion closer to AI chips with 30% better efficiency and 50% smaller power footprint in data-center racks.
why: Deep MIT IP from a decade of research in the Palacios Group (world-leading GaN lab). Addresses a real bottleneck as AI data-center power demand surges. Playground Global lead suggests hard-tech pedigree recognition.
signal: MIT professor co-founder, Palacios Lab IP, Playground Global lead, JIMCO + Shin-Etsu Chemical strategic investors

tagline: AI scientists discovering the next semiconductor materials
founders: Akash (Stanford PhD in material discovery for semiconductors; materials he discovered for nanoscale interconnects adopted into Intel and TSMC roadmaps) + team from Stanford, CMU, IIT-Madras
what: YC W26 company building AI scientists that autonomously discover new semiconductor materials, starting with nanoscale interconnect alloys adopted on Intel and TSMC process roadmaps.
why: Rare combination: Stanford PhD founder whose actual research has already shipped into major foundry roadmaps, plus YC W26 — a batch where 35% scored in YC's all-time top 20%. Deep-tech founder + proven industry adoption is hard to find at seed.
signal: Stanford PhD, materials adopted by Intel/TSMC, YC W26

tagline: Cursor for physical AI — simulation platform for robotics and autonomy
founders: Harry Mellsop (CEO, ex-Tesla Autopilot vision), Alex Langshur (ex-Deep Grey; sold Transpose to Chainalysis), Colton Swingle (ex-Google DeepMind, large-scale model validation infra), Collin Schlager (ex-Meta Reality Labs, Neural Band foundational research) — Stanford grads
what: Simulation platform and digital-twin infrastructure that lets robotics teams build, test, and iterate autonomous systems in one environment, closing the sim-to-real gap for physical AI.
why: Founders' CVs map exactly to the problem: Tesla Autopilot, DeepMind infra, Meta Reality Labs, plus a prior exit. $8.5M seed at $60M post from A*/Category Ventures on top of a $4.25M pre-seed. Still well below the $30M cap.
signal: Stanford grads, ex-Tesla Autopilot/DeepMind/Meta Reality Labs, prior exit, A*/Category Ventures

tagline: Autonomous maritime systems for dual-use defense and blue economy
founders: Luciano Belviso (CEO, previously built Blackshape, acquired by Angel Holding), Luca Mascaro (founder of Sketchin, acquired by BIP Group), Davide Dattoli (founder of Talent Garden)
what: European AI robotics lab building autonomous vessels and control systems for complex maritime environments, with dual-use defense applications across the Mediterranean.
why: Serial-entrepreneur founding team with two prior exits applied to an underbuilt, geopolitically important category (maritime autonomy). Primo Capital/Techshop/40Jemz-led — one of the largest Italian deep-tech pre-seeds.
signal: Serial founders with 2 prior exits, defense-adjacent, Primo Capital lead

tagline: America's Golden Dome for drones
founders: Jason (PhD, Penn State aerospace engineering — aircraft design, supercomputing, ML; previously designed space helicopters for NASA and taught aerospace engineering at Stanford) and Steve (MS, Penn State aerospace; autonomous landing systems at Boeing)
what: YC-backed defense startup building an integrated counter-drone system — 'Golden Dome' — that detects and neutralizes hostile UAVs autonomously for U.S. homeland and base defense.
why: Credentialed aerospace-engineering co-founders with NASA/Boeing/Stanford operating history, tackling the fastest-growing U.S. defense procurement category (counter-UAS).
signal: PhD aerospace founder, NASA/Boeing/Stanford background, YC-backed, Pioneer Fund

tagline: Space-based interceptor satellites for missile defense
founders: Tobias Fischer (Mechanical Engineering, Cornell; previously Astranis) and Sebastian Fischer (Aerospace Engineering, MIT; dual MS/MBA at Harvard; previously NASA, Lockheed Martin, Amazon, Cruise)
what: YC-backed startup building satellite constellations with kinetic interceptors capable of destroying hypersonic and ballistic missiles from orbit. Demoed live ground-based intercepts during YC.
why: MIT/Cornell/Harvard aerospace pedigree with Astranis/NASA/Lockheed experience tackling a clearly articulated $40B/year space-based missile defense market. Live intercept demos during YC are unusual for a 2-person team at this stage.
signal: MIT/Cornell founders, NASA/Lockheed/Astranis background, YC-backed, live intercept demos

tagline: Quantum networking for distributed quantum computing
founders: Manish Singh (PhD '22, UChicago Pritzker — among first U.S. quantum engineering PhDs), Sean Sullivan (PhD), and Supratik Guha (Professor, UChicago Pritzker; senior scientist Argonne National Lab)
what: UChicago/Argonne spinout building quantum network interface controllers, quantum memory modules, and a distributed quantum compiler so heterogeneous quantum computers can be entangled into one fabric.
why: Deep academic + national-lab IP (UChicago Pritzker + Argonne) with a PhD + Professor co-founding team. Total raise still only ~$12M, well under the $30M Series A cap. New CEO (Charles Foley) brings commercial operating experience.
signal: UChicago professor co-founder, Argonne National Lab, Quantonation/Ocean Azul lead

tagline: Gen-AI molecular simulations that replace wet-lab experiments
founders: Prof. Gabor Csanyi (Cambridge), Prof. José Miguel Hernández-Lobato (Cambridge), Dr. Javier Antorán (PhD, Cambridge), Dr. Laurence Midgley (PhD, Cambridge), Henry Ogedegbe — 2 professors + 2 PhDs from Cambridge
what: Cambridge spinout that combines quantum-accurate physics models with generative AI to run molecular simulations matching wet-lab accuracy 100x faster, for pre-clinical drug discovery.
why: Textbook early-stage academic founding team: 2 Cambridge professors + 2 Cambridge PhDs, YC S24, first-author track record on physics-AI ML potentials (Csanyi is a leading figure in ML interatomic potentials). Tiny $500K round — highly accessible entry for a scientifically deep team.
signal: 2 Cambridge professors + 2 PhDs, YC S24, quantum-accurate ML potentials leader (Csanyi), published water-solubility results within wet-lab error range

tagline: Curative gene therapies for kidney and pancreatic diseases
founders: Demetri Maxim (CEO, Stanford computational biologist, PKD patient himself), Vivek Bhalla MD (Stanford nephrologist), and Prof. George Church (Harvard / Wyss Institute — one of the founders of modern synthetic biology)
what: Stanford + Harvard spinout whose NeFIND platform uses AI + high-throughput in-vivo screening to engineer AAV delivery vehicles that reach the kidney and pancreas 10–100x more efficiently, cheaper, and less immunogenic than existing vectors.
why: George Church co-founder plus a mission-driven patient-founder CEO attacking a major delivery bottleneck in gene therapy. Stanford (Mark Kay lab) and Harvard (Church lab) IP in a category (kidney gene therapy) with almost no clinical-stage competitors.
signal: George Church co-founder, Stanford + Harvard spinout, YC, ZAKA VC backing

tagline: Virtual biomarker staining from standard H&E slides
founders: Kenneth To (CEO)
what: Vancouver-based biotech whose AI platform generates per-cell spatial biomarker insights directly from standard H&E pathology slides — no laboratory staining needed — preserving scarce tissue and accelerating trials.
why: Oversubscribed $4.9M seed with Mayo Clinic coming in as a new investor alongside Wittington Ventures, Continuum Health Ventures, Debiopharm, and RiSC. Clinical validation pathway is unusually clear for a seed-stage computational pathology company given Mayo's direct backing.
signal: Mayo Clinic strategic investor, Debiopharm Innovation Fund, Wittington lead

tagline: Monash spinout advancing RAGE-targeted therapeutics to first-in-human
founders: Monash University spinout (scientific founders from Monash immunology / drug discovery groups)
what: Monash University drug-discovery spinout raising Series A to begin first-in-human clinical trials of its lead RAGE-pathway program, led by IP Group and Hostplus.
why: University-spinout Series A at a size (~$19M) that keeps it comfortably under the $30M total-raised cap while already clearing a credible IND hurdle — a rare profile. IP Group lead signals institutional validation of the Monash IP.
signal: Monash University spinout, IP Group lead, Hostplus co-lead, clearing IND-enabling

tagline: Autonomous robots for offshore inspection and maritime infrastructure — RaaS model, no CAPEX
founders: Jean Crosetti (commercial/ops) & Patricia Apostol (CTO, ex-NASA, ETH Zürich engineer; Entrepreneurs First cohort)
what: Bubble deploys swarms of autonomous underwater and surface robots for offshore wind, maritime security and subsea inspection — no upfront CAPEX or offshore mobilisation, full operational capability on day one.
why: ETH Zürich + NASA co-founder at a $5M pre-seed is maximum early-stage access. Emerged from stealth with $4M+ in signed LOIs — real pull from offshore wind operators growing at >20% CAGR. Norrsken Evolve (impact-focused) + Episode 1 give global reach.
signal: Patricia Apostol (ex-NASA, ETH Zürich); Entrepreneurs First; Episode 1 Ventures + Norrsken Evolve; $4M+ LOIs on day 1

tagline: AI-native search infrastructure processing 1M+ queries per second for LLM agents
founders: Kuan Zou (CEO/founder) — Singapore & SF; backed by a syndicate of AI researchers as angels
what: Octen is a purpose-built search engine for AI agents, providing real-time web retrieval infrastructure capable of >1 million queries per second per customer account — latency and throughput that general search APIs cannot deliver.
why: Retrieval is the bottleneck layer as AI agents proliferate. Octen is 3 months old and already raised $10M — Square Peg (top-tier global VC) conviction at inception signals rare infrastructure timing. AI researcher angel syndicate provides technical credibility.
signal: Square Peg lead (Argor co-lead); AI researcher angels; 3-month-old company at $10M seed; 1M+ QPS per customer

tagline: University of Edinburgh spinout converting surplus renewables into clean industrial heat up to 1200°C
founders: Dr. Adam Robinson (inventor/co-founder, University of Edinburgh School of Engineering) & Markus Rondé (CEO)
what: Exergy3 stores surplus renewable electricity as ultra-high-temperature thermal energy (up to 1200°C) and delivers it to industrial processes — decarbonising cement, steel, glass and chemicals while simultaneously balancing the grid.
why: Industrial heat is ~20% of global CO2 and one of the hardest sectors to decarbonise. University of Edinburgh IP provides real technical moat. UK DESNZ government grant + Scottish Enterprise + Old College Capital (Edinburgh's in-house VC) all validate the science before commercial capital arrived.
signal: University of Edinburgh spinout (Dr. Adam Robinson); UK DESNZ grant; Scottish Enterprise; Old College Capital; Axeleo Capital lead

tagline: Software platform for building AI agents and orchestrations on physical gadgets and wearables
founders: Liz Dorman (CEO, ex-Humane — led AI orchestration; joined HP post-acquisition), Alex Ollman (CTO, ex-HP agentic enterprise frameworks), Megan Gole (CPO)
what: Era provides the full software layer for AI hardware makers — voice, intelligence, orchestration, custom model creation — so device builders can embed AI agents into wearables, smart speakers and IoT products without building the stack from scratch.
why: Nobody owns the platform layer for AI hardware and the category is wide open after Humane's pivot. Liz Dorman has direct AI orchestration-at-Humane experience — the highest-signal background for this exact problem. Abstract Ventures + Mozilla Ventures add credibility.
signal: Abstract Ventures + BoxGroup lead; ex-Humane AI orchestration founder; Collaborative Fund, Mozilla Ventures; Flickr co-founder + iPhone keyboard creator as angels

tagline: Agentic AI platform that codifies retiring expert worker knowledge into autonomous agents for critical infrastructure
founders: Lia Reich (CEO), Tyler Collins & Patrick Lohman — operators focused on energy and infrastructure digitisation
what: Cloneable 'shadows' experts in heavy industries — energy, oil & gas, agriculture — capturing audio, documentation and workflow context in real time, then converts that tacit expertise into AI agents that execute the same tasks at scale before the knowledge leaves with retiring workers.
why: The energy sector faces a critical skills cliff as experienced workers retire. Cloneable addresses a $100B+ knowledge-preservation problem. Congruent Ventures (the leading climate/energy technology VC) led — the strongest possible sector credibility signal.
signal: Congruent Ventures lead (top climate/energy VC); First In, Overline participation; critical infrastructure knowledge-transfer moat

tagline: AI agents that continuously track company performance against strategic goals in real time
founders: Chris Pitchford (CEO, founded Ally — Microsoft acquired 2021) & Vic Hu (CTO, ex-Senior Engineer Meta, ex-Engineering Manager Indeed)
what: Brev builds AI agents that monitor company performance against strategic goals around the clock — surfacing misalignments, flagging risks and recommending corrective actions, integrating across the business tools organisations already use.
why: Pitchford's prior company Ally was acquired by Microsoft precisely for this category, giving him proven domain expertise, deep operator relationships and a clearly validated thesis. Ex-Meta CTO adds engineering credibility. $3.3M pre-seed is maximum entry-point accessibility.
signal: Resolute Ventures lead; Microsoft-acquired (Ally) serial founder; ex-Meta Senior Engineer CTO; ShuckerVC, Gaingels, FOG Ventures

tagline: AI context graph and agents automating complex enterprise revenue workflows — RFPs, security questionnaires, business cases
founders: Mikko Mäntylä (CEO, ex-President of Slush — Europe's largest startup event), Miika Huttunen & Johan Jern (all ex-Slush leadership), Helsinki
what: Realm builds a living context graph of a company's go-to-market activities and deploys AI agents on top to handle document-heavy revenue tasks: RFPs, security questionnaires, and business-case generation — the workflows that consume enterprise sales teams' most valuable time.
why: Backed by Slack co-founder Cal Henderson and Deel co-founder Alex Bouaziz as angels — two of the best enterprise operator signals possible. Frontline Ventures + HubSpot Ventures gives commercial distribution. RFP automation is a proven $50B+ cost centre waiting for a native-AI solution.
signal: Frontline Ventures + HubSpot Ventures lead; Slack co-founder + Deel co-founder angels; Slush network; April 2026

tagline: Virtual AI lab running biological foundation models at pharma scale for reproducible in-silico drug discovery
founders: Rick Schneider (ex-Amazon, ex-Celonis), Maxime Allard (PhD — RL & robotics; ex-IBM data science lead) & Mathieu Klop (cardiologist and genomics researcher)
what: Helical builds the middleware between biological foundation models and pharma R&D workflows — a virtual lab platform that lets drug discovery teams run, compare and reproduce AI-driven experiments at production scale without rebuilding infrastructure for every project.
why: Bio foundation models are exploding but remain disconnected from real pharma decisions. Helical is the bridge — backed by Redalpine (top European deep-science VC) plus Cohere CEO Aidan Gomez and Hugging Face CEO Clément Delangue as angels. The AI model ecosystem backing its own drug discovery infrastructure is a remarkable signal.
signal: Redalpine lead; Gradient + BoxGroup; Cohere CEO + Hugging Face CEO as angels; PhD RL/robotics co-founder; cardiologist co-founder

tagline: Johns Hopkins spinout with FDA-cleared noninvasive test for life-threatening invasive fungal infections
founders: Dr. Kieren Marr (CEO/founder — Johns Hopkins infectious disease professor; came out of retirement to lead the company after decades of fungal research)
what: Pearl has developed and FDA-cleared a noninvasive diagnostic test for invasive fungal infections — a leading cause of death in immunocompromised patients — replacing dangerous invasive biopsies with a simple breath or biofluid test. CE-marked for European sales.
why: Hopkins pedigree + FDA clearance + CE mark at Series A is exceptional clinical de-risking. Fungal infections kill ~1.5M people annually with widely inadequate diagnostics; Pearl is commercialising proven science. NIH, NSF and Cystic Fibrosis Foundation grant validation preceded venture capital.
signal: Johns Hopkins University spinout; FDA clearance + CE Mark; NIH/NSF/CFF grant funded; Series A for US + EU commercialisation

tagline: AI infrastructure layer unifying healthcare workforce systems to deploy agents across real clinical roles
founders: Craig Allan Ahrens (CEO, healthcare startup scaler), Michael Biggs (CCO) & Harvey Hongwei Li, PhD (CTO — ex-AI Lead at Uber and Airbnb)
what: Worki provides a unifying infrastructure layer connecting fragmented workforce systems in health systems, enabling AI agents to be deployed across real clinical and administrative roles with humans in control — already live with BJC Healthcare, Tanner Health and other major systems.
why: Healthcare faces millions of unfilled roles and systems that cannot interoperate. CTO Harvey Hongwei Li PhD built and scaled AI infrastructure at Uber and Airbnb — consumer-grade infra credibility brought to healthcare's most urgent workforce problem. Redesign Health (specialist VC) + Tanner Health direct investment.
signal: Redesign Health lead; Tanner Health (Healthliant Ventures); Harvey Hongwei Li PhD (ex-Uber/Airbnb AI Lead) CTO; live with BJC Healthcare

tagline: AGI lab from the creator of Keras
founders: François Chollet (creator of Keras, designer of ARC-AGI benchmark, ex-Google Brain), Mike Knoop (co-founder Zapier)
what: Frontier AGI research lab focused on building AI systems with general intelligence beyond LLM scaling
why: Chollet is one of the most respected ML researchers globally — created Keras, designed ARC-AGI, has a clear contrarian thesis on AGI architecture that justifies the $43M raise on top of YC. Best-of-batch in W26.
signal: YC W26 + creator of Keras + ARC-AGI benchmark designer
source: Extruct AI / TechCrunch YC W26 coverage

tagline: Robots that run autonomous depots for autonomous fleets
founders: Zinny Weli (CEO, ex-Amazon Astro charging at Lab126, ex-Zipline P2 autonomous drone charging lead, Stanford ME, Michigan BSME summa cum laude), Celine Wang (CTO, ex-Plus autonomous trucking mechatronics, Stanford ME)
what: Builds robots that automate depot operations — charging, vehicle checks, maintenance — for electric and autonomous vehicle fleets
why: Founders literally built the predecessor product (Zipline P2 autonomous drone charging towers) at scale. Direct domain mapping rare in robotics — and they pick the bottleneck of the AV economy.
signal: YC W26 + Stanford ME + Amazon Astro / Zipline operator pedigree
source: YC W26 Demo Day / YC Tier List

tagline: Robot cowboys that herd cattle with AI drones
founders: Sam Rogers (grew up on a cattle station, published ML/robotics research from age 15 with CSIRO and the Australian Centre for Robotics, dropped out of university at 18)
what: Converts off-the-shelf drones into autonomous mustering robots that herd cattle, track weight, and monitor land
why: Solo deep-domain founder solving an unloved real-world problem with hard tech. Already deployed and revenue-generating in 90 days. The kind of YC bet that compounds quietly.
signal: YC W26 + CSIRO/ACFR research published since age 15
source: YC W26 Demo Day / Startup Daily

tagline: Post-training research at exabyte scale
founders: Pragya Saboo (ex-Asana, ex-Oscar Health product leadership, Georgia Tech ISE)
what: Builds the human + computational layer that turns expert judgment into training signals for frontier models and agents
why: Rubric sits on the picks-and-shovels side of the post-training arms race. As frontier labs commodify pre-training, evaluation/RLHF infrastructure becomes the choke point — and YC backed three such teams in W26.
signal: YC W26 + adjacent to Ndea/Confluence in YC's AGI cluster

tagline: AI grand strategy game — play any moment in history
founders: YC W26 founding team (technical AI + games background)
what: Alternate-history sandbox where players use generative agents to rewrite key historical scenarios; 35K daily users, viral W26 launch
why: Most-liked W26 launch tweet (2,334 likes), already 35K DAU. Demonstrates a real product-market fit for generative-agent native games — a category most thought wouldn't work yet.
signal: YC W26 + highest virality of the batch + 35K DAU at Demo Day
source: YC Tier List / W26 Demo Day coverage

tagline: Edge-native nervous system for physical AI
founders: Natalya Lopareva (CEO & co-founder)
what: Physics-based predictive safety AI that lets robots perceive human presence and intent at the edge — KUKA partnership for first 'intuitive' industrial robot
why: Robot safety is the gating constraint for human–robot co-working. Algorized's physics-based approach, combined with the KUKA partnership announced at CES 2026, gives them a clear industrial wedge into a $13M round backed by the Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund.
signal: Series A led by Run Ventures + Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund + KUKA collaboration

tagline: 'Smart' antibody-drug conjugates with logic-gate targeting
founders: Daniel Chen, MD/PhD (ex-Genentech for >10 years, led clinical development of Tecentriq — first approved PD-L1 inhibitor; ex-CMO IGM Biosciences), Ramesh Baliga, PhD (CSO, co-founder)
what: Next-generation ADC platform (SYNTHBODY) engineered with logic gates — payload only delivered when the right combination of binding sites is activated, targeting 10x improvement in delivery selectivity
why: Chen is a giant of cancer immunotherapy and ADC design. The logic-gate ADC concept addresses the core safety/selectivity problem holding back the entire ADC field. Backed by Playground Global and Godfrey Capital. Unveiled at AACR April 2026.
signal: Ex-Genentech leader of Tecentriq + Playground Global + AACR launch
source: Fierce Biotech / Endpoints News / Business Wire

tagline: siRNA-powered cell reset therapeutics for longevity
founders: Janine Sengstack, PhD (CSO, scientific founder, ex-UCSF), John Hoekman, PhD (CEO, prior founder/CEO of Impel Pharma — Trudhesa migraine drug), Rob Cahill (COO, ex-UCSF ML researcher, prior Jhana exit)
what: Cell reset therapeutics using siRNA-driven cellular reprogramming to restore youthful function in aged cells; first-in-human trials expected H2 2026
why: Founders combine UCSF science with a CEO who has actually shipped an FDA-approved drug — rare in longevity. The siRNA + reprogramming combo is a more tractable path than direct epigenetic reprogramming. Goldcrest + Godfrey-led $20M.
signal: UCSF spinout + ex-Impel Pharma exited founder + Goldcrest/Godfrey

tagline: Foundation models for orthopedic surgical planning
founders: YC W26 founding team (orthopedic surgery + ML)
what: Foundation model trained on orthopedic imaging and surgical workflows that automates pre-surgical planning, implant sizing, and intra-op decision support
why: Orthopedic planning is high-volume, reimbursed, and currently dominated by manual workflows from incumbents like Stryker. A foundation-model native player can win the planning layer first, then move into intra-op guidance.
signal: YC W26 health + clear payer-aligned wedge
source: YC W26 Health Tech Field Notes

tagline: Multimodal foundation models for patient biology
founders: YC W26 founding team (computational biology + ML)
what: Builds multimodal foundation models that integrate omics, imaging, and clinical data into a unified patient representation for drug discovery and clinical decision support
why: The 'patient foundation model' thesis is the right level of abstraction for AI in medicine — most current biotech AI is single-modality. If they nail the data integration, they become an indispensable layer for both pharma and providers.
signal: YC W26 bio + multimodal foundation-model thesis
source: YC W26 Demo Day biotech roundup
